sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Shortness of breath following exercise.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports shortness of breath following a three-hour bike ride over the weekend. The patient denies chest pain. The patient reports a cough in the past year and a half. The patient reports migraines under control with medication and avoidance of bright lights. The patient reports reflux is managed with protonix. The patient reports cataract surgery approximately four and a half months ago with good vision.
Findings
The clinician observes perfect oxygenation. The clinician observes a regular heart rhythm. The clinician observes no jugular venous distension. The clinician observes a slight wheeze bilaterally. The clinician observes trace lower extremity edema.
Assessment
A possible asthma exacerbation from exercise and allergies is clinically suspected.
Plan
The clinician prescribes an albuterol inhaler, two puffs every four hours as needed. The clinician orders a pulmonary referral. The clinician plans to continue imitrex as needed for migraines. The clinician wants to see the patient in two weeks if shortness of breath recurs.
